title: Rorippa microtites
taxonomy: Rorippa microtites is classified as a species within the plant family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. It belongs to the genus Rorippa, a group of plants often referred to as watercress or yellow cress. Rorippa microtites is a flowering plant native to North America. The classification of Rorippa microtites places it in the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. As a member of the Brassicaceae family, it shares characteristics with other plants in this group, such as mustard and horseradish.
